commit.message:
fix: downgrade SignalR.Client to 9.x and simplify Blazor routing

- Root cause: Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client 10.0.3 was incompatible
  with .NET 9 runtime — the MessagePack blazorpack protocol's
  IInvocationBinder.GetTarget(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) method doesn't exist in
  the 9.x server assemblies, causing immediate circuit disconnect.
- Downgraded to SignalR.Client 9.0.6 to match the .NET 9 target framework.
- Simplified Routes.razor to always use RouteView (no AuthorizeRouteView),
  since page-level auth gating is handled at the API layer.
- Updated Program.cs auth check to skip placeholder ClientId values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(run): smart resume — skip DONE phases, reset only FAILED/PENDING

POST /api/missions/{id}/run now:
- Resets FAILED phases → PENDING (re-executed)
- Leaves DONE/DONE_WITH_ISSUES phases untouched (skipped by orchestrator)
- Accepts optional from_phase (int) to hard-reset from a phase index
- Resets run status from completed/failed/paused → running

This means restarting a failed run no longer replays ideation/comite-strat
that already succeeded — only the broken phase reruns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

across the frontend, backend, and admin services.

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

fix(auth): demo login fails with Invalid credentials when hash is stale

When the demo user already exists but was created with a different
password (e.g. prior session, env change), register() is silently
skipped and login() fails because the stored bcrypt hash doesn't match.

Fix: when register() raises AuthError (email_taken), call
force_reset_password() to update the hash before attempting login.
The new force_reset_password() function is intentionally minimal and
internal — only called by demo_login.

- Excessive API use (no search debounce, IMVDB N+1 calls)
- Navigation fluidity (StateFlow collection inefficiencies)
- UX indicator state (optimistic update patterns, empty listener setup)
- Trakt implementation (both auth flows, broken refresh, scrobble coverage)
- Trakt-free operation (fully supported via local DB and cinemeta fallback)
- AIOStreams-free operation (6 preset providers + any Stremio addon)
- Per-user isolation (music_library/podcast PK bug, missing debrid backup)

commit.message:
Fix countdown sync, double-goal, and audio overload

- Countdown sync: replace 'only-increase' guard with score-sum round guard.
  Stale pre-goal packets (recv_score_sum < local) are rejected; current-round
  packets sync countdown freely at 60Hz, eliminating fast/slow drift.

- Double goal: score moved outside auth gate (max-only, always safe);
  countdown accept gated on fresh_round to prevent stale countdown=0 from
  clearing a post-goal countdown=2.5.

- 60Hz sends: removed 30Hz throttle (net_tick); send every frame (~14KB/s).

- Packet-loss recovery: isHostAuth in every packet + prev_recv_host_auth
  tracks authority transitions; auth_changed flag forces puck acceptance
  on the first packet after any handoff, regardless of consecutive losses.

- Audio suite: rewrote audio.ts with 9 sound functions (hit, wall, goal,
  near-miss, countdown tick/go, win/lose); near-miss tightened to goal-gap
  + 12px threshold + 2s cooldown to prevent AudioContext overload.

- F1 countdown lights: 5 red lamps illuminating one-by-one before game start.

- QR camera selection: labeled buttons for all cameras with localStorage
  persistence (key: qr_camera_id).

- Opponent paddle interpolation increased 0.25 -> 0.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add network bandwidth (Mbps) to VM comparison

Azure VM SKU API does not expose network bandwidth - sourced from the
public MicrosoftDocs/azure-compute-docs GitHub repo at cache refresh time.

Backend (function_app.py):
- fetch_network_bandwidth(): gets full file tree in one GitHub API call,
  then concurrently fetches ~150 *-series.md files via raw.githubusercontent.com
  (CDN, no rate limit), parses the ### [Network] tab tables, returns
  Dict[str, int] of SKU name -> max bandwidth in Mbps
- refresh_sku_cache(): calls fetch_network_bandwidth() once before parallel
  region processing, passes result into refresh_region()
- refresh_region(): adds networkBandwidthMbps field to each cached entity
- extract_capabilities_from_sku(): reads networkBandwidthMbps from sku/cache
- calculate_similarity(): uses networkBandwidthMbps for network scoring
  when available; falls back to maxNics for SKUs without bandwidth data
- calculate_differences(): adds networkBandwidthMbps to network diff section

Frontend (app.js):
- formatBandwidth(): displays as Gbps (>=1000 Mbps) or Mbps
- displayTargetSku(): shows Network Bandwidth when available
- Network diff section: shows Max Bandwidth row above Max NICs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

👪 Adds support for PBCore JSON Schema using Pydantic models and more.

* Adds a full suite of Pydantic models for representing all PBCore elements.
* Adds Python notebooks for demonstrations, debugging, and testing.
* Changes XSL stylesheet for XML to JSON conversion
  * Completes the list of repeatable elements.
  * Explicitly enumerate which elements have child elements; for simplifying.
* Utility for re-generating JSON schema file from the Pydantic models.
  * Pydantic models are the source-of-truth for the JSON schema file.
  * JSON schema file is the official published PBCore JSON schema.
  * When making changes
  * NOTE: PBCore community has not mandated or approved official JSON schema yet.
    This is mainly for GBH use for now, but may propose to PBCore community later.
* Adds pytest for testing the JSON and XML PBCore
  * Tests include factory methods and test data.
  * Tests Pydantic validation as adhering to the PBCore schema.
  * Tests for any changes from previously generated JSON schema file.
  * Tests invalid PBCore XML and non-xml.
  * Testing XML to JSON conversion for:
    * always outputting JSON arrays for repeatable elements
    * ensure multiple repeatable elements are grouped properly
    * consistent output with or without attributes
  * Reorganizes test code
    * Mpves main.py into app/main.py and changes Dockerfile command accordingly.
    * Move test_main.py into a 'tests' package directory.
    * Add files used for test fixtures in 'tests/sample_data/' directory.
    * Adds 'test' service for running pytest tests with docker compose.
      * Usage: `docker compose run --rm -it test`
* Adds tools for VSCode developers including:
  * Devcontainers config in .devcontainers/, which runs VSCode within the
    container built by the Dockerfile. Saves a lot of debugging time!
  * .vscode/ directory for VSCode config including:
    * launch.json for running the FastAPI app locally.
    * settings.json to use pytest with VSCode test runner/debugger.
* Updates Github Actins
  * run test
  * build image
* Updates README with how to run tests.
* Adds missing 'raise' for HTTPException
* Upgrades uv.lock
  * Specifically, upgrades lxml from 6.0.0 to 6.0.2 to bring in some bug fixes that will hopefully solve build error re: libxml2 in Github Actions.
* Adds a safe way of accepting URLs as input for conversion/validation.
  * Adds module `security/url_fetch.py` which includes function `safe_fetch_url`.
  * Modifies FastAPI path operations to use safe_fetch_url instead of httpx.Client.get directly.
  * Uses FastAPI Depends for adding a safe URL dependency for path ops that take URLs.
  * Uses HttpUrl for type validation on the URL for path ops that take URLs.
* Fixes typos and linting errors with Ruff and Black

commit.message:
Fix model generating no reply despite high vector similarity confidence (#53)

* Initial plan

* Fix model text generation silently failing due to low maxOutputTokens

When runGroundedAnswerAgent generated a detailed HTML answer embedded in
JSON, the 700-token cap caused the output to be truncated mid-JSON.
parseLLMJSON then threw a parse error that was silently caught and
converted to {status:'NO_INFORMATION'}, dropping the reply even when
vector similarity was 80-90%. Fix: raise the cap to 2000 tokens and add
console.error logging so future parse failures are visible in logs.

commit.message:
fix: MCP watchdog fcntl lock — prevent double-manager race on blue/green slots

When two uvicorn slots (blue:8090 + green:8091) run on the same node, both
would start MCP and kill each other in a 30s loop. Fix:
- fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB) at startup — only one slot becomes manager
- Watchdog re-checks lock on every 15s tick, exits if lock lost
- node-1: macaron-platform-green disabled (nginx only uses :8090)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: thinking tokens shown as collapsible + accurate rate limits

Two features:

   <think>...</think> tags is displayed in a collapsible 💭 widget
   (like tool calls), with a preview in the title. The actual
   response content renders normally below. Status bar shows

   - models-catalog.json: all Azure AI models (DeepSeek, Grok,
     Kimi) now have rate_limits defaults
   - config.json: updated with actual S0 limits from probing
     every deployment's x-ratelimit headers
   - gpt-4o family: RPM=20, TPM=20K (capacity increase worked)
   - gpt-5/5.1/o-series: RPM=1-10, TPM=1K (S0 hard caps)
